content.claim: Tracking in Hungary starts early compared to OECD average, partially at Grade 5 (age 10-11) or later at Grade 7 (age 12-13)
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Describes structural timing, not demonstrated causal harm to equity outcomes
Does not differentiate impact of 6/8-year tracks from other tracking mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
